title: Buttery Flaky Ethiopian Squid Crumble
description: A romantic and indulgent vegetarian-friendly dish featuring tenderized squid baked in a buttery flaky crust for an authentic African taste.

ingredients.name: Vegetable stock cube (optional, for flavoring the fish)

instructions: Place the firm white fish fillets in a large mixing bowl.
Add 1/2 cup of flour and 1 tsp each of salt and black pepper. Mix thoroughly to coat all the pieces evenly with the spice mixture.
Heat vegetable oil in a pan over medium heat, add the seasoned fish fillets on both sides until golden brown and cooked through (about 5 minutes per side).
Drain excess oil from the fish and set aside.
Place 6 oz of fresh squid pieces into another mixing bowl. Add 1/2 cup flour to coat the squid evenly.
Stir in vegetable stock cube if using, ensuring all pieces are coated well with the spice mixture. Set aside to marinate for about 30 minutes.
Preheat your oven to 400°F (205°C). Transfer the seasoned fish and squid into a single layer on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Pour remaining flour over both layers evenly, ensuring each piece is fully coated. Cover the dish with aluminum foil.
Cook in preheated oven for 30 minutes or until fish and squid are cooked through and golden brown (the crust will be flaky).
Remove from oven, uncover, and let rest for 5-10 minutes before serving hot.
Garnish with fresh herbs if desired.
